SEARCH
Package it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.View.GUI

Class GameBoardController

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.View.GUI.GUIController
it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.View.GUI.GameBoardController
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.fxml.Initializable
public class GameBoardController extends GUIController implements javafx.fxml.Initializable
Controller to manage the game GUI.

    GameBoardController()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addMessageToChat(ChatMessage message)
    Adds a message to the appropriate chat and updates the current chat display if necessary.
    String
    colorButtons(PlayerColor playerColor)
    Determines the color code for styling buttons based on the given player color.
    void
    drawCard(Boolean deckWanted)
    Constructs and sends a DrawMessage to the server based on the specified deckWanted parameter.
    void
    drawGold(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent mouseEvent)
    Handles the event of drawing a gold card by calling the drawCard method with the deckWanted parameter set to false.
    void
    drawResource(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent mouseEvent)
    Handles the event of drawing a resource card by calling the drawCard method with the deckWanted parameter set to true.
    void
    fourthPlayerGrid()
    Displays the grid for the fourth player.
    javafx.scene.image.Image
    getImageBack(String id)
    Retrieves the image of the back side of a card with the specified ID.
    javafx.scene.image.Image
    getImageFront(String id)
    Retrieves the image of the front side of a card with the specified ID.
    void
    initialize(URL url, ResourceBundle resourceBundle)
    Initializes the controller after its root element has been completely processed.
    void
    initializeButtons()
    Initializes the buttons for displaying player grids in the GUI.
    void
    initializeScore()
    Initializes the score display by associating player colors with corresponding pin images.
    void
    pickCard(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent event)
    Handles the event of picking a card from the displayed cards by clicking on its ImageView.
    void
    secondPlayerGrid()
    Displays the grid for the second player.
    void
    sendMessage(javafx.event.ActionEvent event)
    Sends a chat message based on the user's input and updates the chat.
    static void
    setGuiView(GUIView view)
    Sets the GUI view for the application.
    void
    setObjectiveDisplayed()
    Sets the displayed objective cards images.
    void
    setPersonalObjective()
    Sets the image of the personal objective card based on the first card in the player's hand objectives.
    void
    setTokenImage()
    Sets token images based on player scores, using predefined pinGroups and colorImageMap.
    void
    setTurn()
    Sets turn's text based on the current player.
    void
    showGrid(String nickname)
    Displays the grid for a specified player.
    void
    start(javafx.stage.Stage stage)
    Initializes the JavaFX application, loads the GameBoard.fxml file, and sets up the main stage.
    void
    thirdPlayerGrid()
    Displays the grid for the third player.
    void
    toggleChatMenu(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
    Toggles the visibility of the chat pane and updates the button text accordingly.
    void
    updateDisplayedCard()
    Updates the displayed resource and gold cards on the board.
    void
    updateGoldDeck()
    Updates the image of the gold deck card based on the first card in the gold deck.
    void
    updateHand()
    Updates the display of the player's hand cards on the GUI.
    void
    updatePane()
    Updates the game board with rectangles representing available positions for card placement.
    void
    updateResourceDeck()
    Updates the image of the resource deck card based on the first card in the resource deck.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • GameBoardController

      public GameBoardController()

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start(javafx.stage.Stage stage) throws Exception
      Initializes the JavaFX application, loads the GameBoard.fxml file, and sets up the main stage.
      Specified by:
      start in class GUIController
      Parameters:
      stage - The primary stage of the JavaFX application.
      Throws:
      Exception - If there is an error during initialization or loading of resources.

    • setGuiView

      public static void setGuiView(GUIView view)
      Sets the GUI view for the application.
      Parameters:
      view - the GUIView instance to be set

    • initialize

      public void initialize(URL url, ResourceBundle resourceBundle)
      Initializes the controller after its root element has been completely processed. This method is called once all FXML elements have been processed and are ready to use. It sets initial values, initializes GUI components, and sets up event handlers.
      Specified by:
      initialize in interface javafx.fxml.Initializable
      Parameters:
      url - The location used to resolve relative paths for the root object, or null if the location is not known.
      resourceBundle - The resources used to localize the root object, or null if the resource is not found.

    • initializeButtons

      public void initializeButtons()
      Initializes the buttons for displaying player grids in the GUI. Sets up button styles and text based on player colors and nicknames.

    • colorButtons

      public String colorButtons(PlayerColor playerColor)
      Determines the color code for styling buttons based on the given player color. Returns a hexadecimal color code corresponding to the specified player color.
      Parameters:
      playerColor - The color of the player for whom the button style is determined.
      Returns:
      A string representing the hexadecimal color code for the button style.

    • setTurn

      public void setTurn()
      Sets turn's text based on the current player.
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if an IOException occurs while sending the message to retrieve available colors

    • updateHand

      public void updateHand()
      Updates the display of the player's hand cards on the GUI. Retrieves the current hand cards from the virtual view and updates the corresponding ImageView components with the front images of the cards.

    • setPersonalObjective

      public void setPersonalObjective()
      Sets the image of the personal objective card based on the first card in the player's hand objectives. Retrieves the ID of the card, formats it, and sets the corresponding front image to the ImageView component.

    • updateGoldDeck

      public void updateGoldDeck()
      Updates the image of the gold deck card based on the first card in the gold deck. Retrieves the ID of the card, formats it, and sets the corresponding back image to the ImageView component.

    • updateResourceDeck

      public void updateResourceDeck()
      Updates the image of the resource deck card based on the first card in the resource deck. Retrieves the ID of the card, formats it, and sets the corresponding back image to the ImageView component.

    • updateDisplayedCard

      public void updateDisplayedCard()
      Updates the displayed resource and gold cards on the board.

    • setObjectiveDisplayed

      public void setObjectiveDisplayed()
      Sets the displayed objective cards images.

    • getImageBack

      public javafx.scene.image.Image getImageBack(String id)
      Retrieves the image of the back side of a card with the specified ID.
      Parameters:
      id - The ID of the card for which the back image is requested.
      Returns:
      The Image object representing the back side of the card.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the specified resource for the card back image is null.

    • getImageFront

      public javafx.scene.image.Image getImageFront(String id)
      Retrieves the image of the front side of a card with the specified ID.
      Parameters:
      id - The ID of the card for which the front image is requested.
      Returns:
      The Image object representing the front side of the card.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the specified resource for the card front image is null.

    • initializeScore

      public void initializeScore()
      Initializes the score display by associating player colors with corresponding pin images.

    • setTokenImage

      public void setTokenImage()
      Sets token images based on player scores, using predefined pinGroups and colorImageMap.

    • pickCard

      public void pickCard(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent event) throws IOException
      Handles the event of picking a card from the displayed cards by clicking on its ImageView. Constructs and sends a PickMessage to the server with information about the picked card.
      Parameters:
      event - The MouseEvent representing the click event on the ImageView.
      Throws:
      IOException - If there is an error sending the PickMessage.

    • drawResource

      public void drawResource(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent mouseEvent) throws IOException
      Handles the event of drawing a resource card by calling the drawCard method with the deckWanted parameter set to true.
      Parameters:
      mouseEvent - The MouseEvent representing the mouse click event triggering the draw action.
      Throws:
      IOException - If there is an error sending the DrawMessage.

    • drawGold

      public void drawGold(javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent mouseEvent) throws IOException
      Handles the event of drawing a gold card by calling the drawCard method with the deckWanted parameter set to false.
      Parameters:
      mouseEvent - The MouseEvent representing the mouse click event triggering the draw action.
      Throws:
      IOException - If there is an error sending the DrawMessage.

    • drawCard

      public void drawCard(Boolean deckWanted) throws IOException
      Constructs and sends a DrawMessage to the server based on the specified deckWanted parameter.
      Parameters:
      deckWanted - A boolean indicating whether the resource deck (true) or gold deck (false) is to be drawn from.
      Throws:
      IOException - If there is an error sending the DrawMessage.

    • updatePane

      public void updatePane()
      Updates the game board with rectangles representing available positions for card placement. Rectangles are centered on the pane and styled. Handles drag-and-drop events for card placement. Clears and updates rectangleCoordinateHashMap with current coordinates.

    • secondPlayerGrid

      public void secondPlayerGrid()
      Displays the grid for the second player.

    • thirdPlayerGrid

      public void thirdPlayerGrid()
      Displays the grid for the third player.

    • fourthPlayerGrid

      public void fourthPlayerGrid()
      Displays the grid for the fourth player.

    • showGrid

      public void showGrid(String nickname)
      Displays the grid for a specified player. The grid consists of images representing cards placed at specific coordinates. Adjusts the scroll pane to focus on the last card placed.
      Parameters:
      nickname - The nickname of the player whose grid is to be displayed.

    • addMessageToChat

      public void addMessageToChat(ChatMessage message)
      Adds a message to the appropriate chat and updates the current chat display if necessary.
      Parameters:
      message - the ChatMessage to be added to the chat

    • sendMessage

      public void sendMessage(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws IOException
      Sends a chat message based on the user's input and updates the chat.
      Parameters:
      event - the ActionEvent triggered by the user action
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while sending the message

    • toggleChatMenu

      public void toggleChatMenu(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      Toggles the visibility of the chat pane and updates the button text accordingly.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - the ActionEvent triggered by the user action
